title: Social Worker (KIP)
description:
COMPANY DESCRIPTION

The Salvation Army has been touching lives in Singapore since 1935 and we remain committed in serving the underprivileged in the community without discrimination. To us, every life is precious and we actively help those in need regardless of age, race or religion.

Throughout the years, we have established a comprehensive network of social services to meet a wide range of needs in our community. Our diverse programmes reach out to children who need care and protection, families in material need, ailing aged requiring quality nursing care and other needy segments in our society.

Our spiritual ministry also reaches out to the people in the community to provide counsel to those in need. We are there to help guide those in need towards finding their identity and purpose in life.

RESPONSIBILITIES

About Us

The Children and Youth Group (CYG) in The Salvation Army Singapore serves hundreds of vulnerable children and youth yearly. In line with our vision to provide a continuum of care to uplift those in need, our services span from preventive services, foster care and residential services to reunification support services for vulnerable children returning to their natural families after being removed due to safety concerns. We believe that every child is precious and unique and children grow best in families. We also believe in the potential of every child and family to transform and thrive.

Kids In Play is one of our key programmes and it aims to support and empower families and children who have a parent in prison. We provide professional support to address the impact of parental incarceration on children and caregivers, help to strengthen familial bonds and facilitate the reintegration of incarcerated persons with their families. Our vision is to build an inclusive society where families of incarcerated persons are embraced, thriving and empowered to be contributing members of society.


About the job

As a Social Worker at Kids In Play, you are expected to provide social work services and therapeutic interventions to children, their care-givers and incarcerated parents. You are also expected to work in tandem with the Kids In Play staff team in delivering group work and family bonding programmes to families registered on the programme.


MAIN DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:

Casework Management:

  • Register and orientate new families through the intake process, and provide information and referral services.
  • Provide casework and counselling services through home visits and prison visits as part of the intervention plan. This includes meeting children and their care-givers in their family contexts, and inmates inside prisons for the purpose of intervention work. Aftercare services are also to be rendered to inmates after release from prison with the purpose of reintegration and reunification with their children and families.
  • Able to utilize therapeutic approaches and intervention strategies in working with children, caregivers, parents, couples and families.
  • Respond appropriately to crisis situations and recommend relevant intervention strategies.
  • When appropriate, participate in and/or conduct case conferences and work with other professionals in rendering help to families.
  • Keep and maintain up-to-date reports, statistics, records, and case reviews.

Volunteer Training:

  • Screen, recruit, train and develop volunteers for programmes as required.
  • Brief and de-brief volunteers regularly as part of training and equipping volunteers

Group Work:

  • Plan and facilitate group work sessions for children and youth.
  • Plan and facilitate caregiver support groups.
  • Plan and execute parent training, and other workshops. These include sessions held for incarcerated parents.

Programme Planning:

  • Plan and execute programmes and activities for children, youth and families that are aligned with the vision and mission of the centre.
  • Conduct review and evaluation of programmes which serves as a resource for fine-tuning future runs of the programmes.

Prison Playroom:

  • Registration of children in the Playroom located at the Prison Link Centre in Changi.
  • Manage and maintain the Playroom in good condition.
  • Build rapport with visiting children and their caregivers.
  • Provide information as part of The Salvation Army-Kids In Play outreach programme to children with incarcerated parents.

Community Work:

  • Collate and analyze needs of our target population of clients.
  • Assess and bridge the gap between stakeholders and beneficiaries.
  • Work with various stakeholders in the community to achieve the vision.

Administration

  • Ensure efficient use of resources and work within approved budgets for all programs and services.
  • Undertake any other administrative duties as may reasonably be required of you by the management team.

QUALIFICATIONS

Qualifications

  • Degree in Social Work from a recognisable university
  • Accredited as Registered Social Worker with SASW.
  • At least 1-2 years in direct social work is preferred.
  • Experience in working with children and youths and their families

Job Requirements

  • Registered Social Worker is preferred
  • Keen to work in children and youth sector
  • A good communicator
  • Great team player
  • Have strong conflict and interpersonal skills

Core Competencies

  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills.
  • Able to connect with children and youth.
  • Social Work practice with vulnerable families and individuals.
  • Mobilisation, activation and management of volunteers and resources.
  • Case management and casework knowledge and skills.
  • Risk assessment and risk management.
  • Good report writing skills.
OTHER INFORMATION

Duty Hours

Total work hours is 44 hours per week.

Under normal circumstances the hours of duty will be as follows:

  • 9am to 6pm (Monday - Friday, 1 hour lunch)

Weekday evenings and Saturdays are usually designated non-working hours, but this is subject to the operational and programme needs of KIP and shall be determined by CYG on exigencies of services rendered to clients.

Work Location

The Salvation Army, Kids In Play

Blk 65 Kallang Bahru #01-305 Singapore 330065

By submitting this job application, I affirm that I have given to The Salvation Army true and complete information on this application. No requested information has been concealed.

I agree that The Salvation Army (TSA) may collect, use and disclose your personal data, as provided in this application form, or (if applicable) obtained by TSA as a result of your employment, for the following purposes in accordance with the Personal Data Protection Act 2012 and our data protection policy (available at our website at https://www.salvationarmy.org.sg/data-protection-policy/ ):

  1. a) the processing of this employment application;
  2. b) the administration of the employment with TSA; and
  3. c) sharing of personal information to third party, including but not limited to government agencies, hospitals, vendor(s)/supplier (s), working in collaboration with TSA to support the employment.

If you are providing third party's personal data, you hereby declare that you have obtained consent from the named individual(s), for the collection, use and disclosure of his/her personal data by you to TSA, TSA business partners and other third-party service providers. I authorised the organisation to contact references provided for employment reference checks.

Please visit our website at https://www.salvationarmy.org.sg/data-protection-policy/ for further details on our data protection policy, including how you may access and correct your personal data or withdraw consent to the collection, use or disclosure of your personal data.

If any information I have provided is untrue, or if I have concealed material information, I understand that this will constitute cause for denial of employment or immediate dismissal.

Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - The Salvation Army Singapore

Throughout the years, we have established a comprehensive network of social services to meet a wide range of needs in or community. If you are looking for a fulfilling career and have a passion for people, we invite you to join us.
